Why no software update can fix it: Every Apple device contains a small permanent piece of code called SecureROM - physically etched into the chip at the factory - that runs before iOS loads. SecureROM cannot be updated after manufacturing, so no patch is really possible.

Secure Enlave is NOT immediately affected. Apple devices contain a separate isolated component, the Secure Enclave, that handles passcodes, Face ID, and encryption keys. This exploit does not directly breach it, so a successful attack is not an instant data dump. It's still pretty bad, though.
